seoTitle: How to Share Microsoft Loop With External Users
metaDescription: Invite clients, freelancers, or outside stakeholders into a Microsoft Loop page or workspace using secure external sharing links and guest access.
intro: Collaborating with people outside your organization in Microsoft Loop requires a slightly different setup than sharing internally. This guide covers sharing an individual page link with an external email, inviting a guest to a full workspace, and what to check if external sharing seems to be blocked.

steps.heading: Confirm external sharing is allowed
steps.body: Open your target workspace and verify your admin has enabled external sharing controls before attempting to invite outside users.

steps.heading: Share an individual page
steps.body: Click the Share button in the top right corner, select the page link option, open link settings, choose 'People you choose,' and enter the guest's external email.

steps.heading: Send the link
steps.body: Copy the generated link and send it to your external stakeholder, which triggers the automatic guest provisioning process.

steps.heading: Share the full workspace if needed
steps.body: For broader access, click Share and select the full workspace invite tab, then enter the guest's email and set their editing permissions.

steps.heading: Guide your guest through access
steps.body: Help your external collaborator understand how to switch tenant views and access your shared pages so they aren't confused by the login process.

faq.question: Can anyone outside my company access a shared Loop page?
faq.answer: Only people you explicitly invite through a page or workspace link can access it — sharing settings must be configured to 'People you choose' for external stakeholders.

faq.question: How long does it take for a guest invite to work?
faq.answer: It can take some time after a page invite for full workspace access to provision, so allow a short delay before troubleshooting access issues.

faq.question: What if external sharing isn't working at all?
faq.answer: This is often due to organization-level admin settings blocking external sharing, so check with your Microsoft 365 or SharePoint admin.

faq.question: Do external guests need a Microsoft account?
faq.answer: Guests typically go through a provisioning process tied to Microsoft Entra ID, which may require them to sign in or create an account depending on your organization's settings.
